assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: The response mentions that multislice jobs cannot be live-migrated and will be terminated, but doesn't specifically explain the atomic recreation behavior or contrast it with rolling upgrades

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: The response clearly states that the training job will be terminated and explains this is due to complex inter-slice communication requirements, implying all slices are affected together

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states 'Complete Training Interruption: Your training job will be terminated' and mentions all 4 slices (1024 TPU chips total) are affected

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: The response mentions 'TPU v5e availability might be limited immediately after maintenance' and discusses 'TPU slice re-allocation time' as part of expected downtime

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response explicitly recommends 'Save an extra checkpoint manually before the maintenance window' in the Best Practices section

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: The response provides checkpoint configuration code and discusses graceful shutdown planning with automatic checkpoint saving

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: The response does not mention maintenance exclusions or the ability to defer maintenance events

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: The response mentions maintenance windows in the YAML configuration example with 'maintenance-window: sunday-03:00-07:00' and discusses timing control
